Let  Δ ABC be any equilateral triangle  AB = CB = AC .
Step one
Join  C to  M , the midpoint of  AB .
Prove  ΔCAM ≡ ΔCBM (SSS) .
 ∴ ∠CAM = ∠CBM (corresponding angles in congruent 
triangles)
Step two
Join  A to  N , the midpoint of  CB .
Prove  ΔANC ≡ ΔANB .
 ∴ ∠ACN = ∠ABN (corresponding angles in congruent 
triangles)
Now  ∠CAB = ∠ABC = ∠ACB 
and as  ∠CAB + ∠ABC + ∠ACB = 180° 
(angle sum of  ΔABC )
 ∠CAB = ∠ABC = ∠ACB = 60°
